metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_486
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Agrin is concentrated in the synaptic cleft of ribbon (Fig. 4) and conventional (Fig. 5) synapses of the inner and outer plexiform layers. As the majority of synapses in plexiform layers are labeled and many of these synapses are glutamatergic (PMID: 7902364) we assume that excitatory glutamateric synapses contain Agrin in the synaptic cleft.

12/6/2017 Pim
- Feedback Gundelfinger lab: "There is perfect evidence that Agrin is part of the cleft ECM. The supporting evidence identifies Agrin as HSPG component of the ECM. We believe that the evidence for our annotation is very strong." Therefore, ECM part of the term is derived from knowledge on the canonical function of the protein.This notion agreed with annotations present in the Uniprot database.. Experimental description: "Fertilized White Leghorn Chicken eggs ...", p. 4189
